mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases, or the Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS change and 20-day return is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.0%, restrictive monetary policy increases discount rates and debt-service costs for corporates, systematically reducing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 2.0%, or the mean return in those instances is not statistically below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts reveal broad deterioration in consumer and residential investment demand, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) relative to individual proxies.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, accelerating industrial production signals capacity constraints and impending input cost inflation, prompting markets to price in accelerated Federal Reserve tightening that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.5%, positive 60-day returns follow INDPRO acceleration in more than 50% of observations, or the rank correlation between INDPRO change and 60-day return is positive and significant (p < 0.05) during that regime.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in headline CPI exceeding 0.3% signals upside inflation risk that raises near-term expectations of Federal Reserve hawkishness, triggering a rapid repricing of equities and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months, or the mean 5-day return after a 0.3% CPI surprise is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A single-month increase in the unemployment rate greater than 0.2 percentage points reveals abrupt labor market weakening that heightens recession risk and drives immediate risk-off flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp in one month
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns follow fewer than 60% of qualifying UNRATE jumps, or the average return in those windows is insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of low unemployment combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates a non-inflationary tight labor market that supports corporate pricing power and margins without monetary headwinds, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations when UNRATE is below 5.0% and FEDFUNDS is flat or down over the prior 3 months, or the interaction term lacks incremental predictive power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP captures persistent aggregate demand dynamics that drive corporate earnings revisions, and this macro momentum is gradually priced into equities over the next quarter, producing positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of quarters with above-median GDP growth.
